preview.summary: Germany face a dangerous Ivory Coast in Group E at BMO Field, pitting Julian Nagelsmann's possession-based side against the highest-valued African squad at the tournament. The Germans boast elite attacking midfield talent, while the Elephants counter with athletic, Premier League-laced quality. A test of German control versus Ivorian transition speed.
preview.homeForm: Ranked 10th by FIFA, rebuilding under Nagelsmann with Manuel Neuer back in goal at 40.
preview.awayForm: Ranked around 34th, topping their CAF group unbeaten with the best goal difference in African qualifying.
preview.homeKey: Florian Wirtz, the creative force in Germany's attack
preview.awayKey: Franck Kessié, the captain leading the Elephants from midfield
storylines: Wirtz and Musiala dovetailing in Germany's final third
Ivory Coast's first World Cup since 2014
Amad Diallo's pace as Ivory Coast's attacking outlet
preview.h2h: The nations have met only in friendlies, with no prior World Cup encounter.
